channel.title: Coffee Drinks on Barista At Home
channel.description: Recent content in Coffee Drinks on Barista At Home

item.title: What Is Nitro Cold Brew? The Creamy, No-Ice Coffee Explained (+ Starbucks Guide)
item.description: Nitro cold brew is cold brew coffee infused with nitrogen gas — smooth, creamy, and naturally sweeter with a velvety cascade pour. Here's what's in it, how Starbucks makes it, and how to make it at home.

item.title: Espresso vs Cappuccino: What's the Difference?
item.description: Espresso is a concentrated 1–2 oz shot of pure coffee. A cappuccino is espresso with steamed milk and thick foam. Here's how they differ in taste, caffeine, calories, and when to drink each.

item.title: Flat White vs Macchiato: What's the Difference?
item.description: A flat white is 5–6 oz of espresso with silky microfoam. A macchiato is a 2 oz espresso with just a dash of milk foam. Here's exactly how they differ in milk, size, strength, and Starbucks versions.

item.title: What Is a Latte? Espresso, Milk Ratios, and Types Explained
item.description: A latte is espresso topped with steamed milk and a thin layer of microfoam. Learn what makes a latte, how ratios work, types of lattes, and how it differs from a cappuccino or flat white.

item.title: Long Black Coffee: What It Is, How to Make It, and How It Differs from an Americano
item.description: Long black coffee is an espresso drink made by pouring a double shot over hot water. Learn what makes it different from an Americano, how to make it at home, and why the pour order matters.

item.title: Cold Foam Recipe: How to Make Cold Foam at Home (3 Methods)
item.description: The easiest cold foam recipe: make cold foam at home in 3 minutes using a frother, mason jar, or blender. Covers sweet cream cold foam, vanilla cold foam, and dairy-free options.
